task_completion.visual_evidence: The row containing MSFT is highlighted or selected in the JStock watchlist.

task_completion.description: Open the price alert dialog for MSFT
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box or window specifically for setting alerts for Microsoft (MSFT) is visible on the screen.
The checkbox or toggle associated with the 'Fall Below' (or similar wording) alert condition is checked or activated.
The value '350' or '350.00' is visible inside the input text field next to the 'Fall Below' condition.

task_completion.description: Save and confirm the price alert
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks 'OK' or 'Save' on the alert dialog, or the dialog is closed and the watchlist shows an active alert indicator (like a bell icon or the price 350) for MSFT.

integrity.description: Agent used the JStock graphical user interface to set the alert.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with JStock menus, dialogs, and buttons rather than terminal commands or scripts modifying configuration files.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual JStock application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JStock application window is clearly visible with its standard interface, not a text editor, browser, or mock interface.

integrity.description: Agent did not just write the task instructions or expected outcome into a text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no open text editors (like Notepad or Gedit) containing the words 'MSFT', 'Fall Below', or '350'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected outcome is that the 'Fall Below' price alert for Microsoft (MSFT) is modified to exactly 350 in the JStock application.
